title: Burden of disease
subtitle: [Disability-Adjusted Life Years](#dod:dalys) (DALYs) per 100,000 individuals from all causes. DALYs measure the total burden of disease – both from years of life lost due to premature death and years lived with a disability. One DALY equals one lost year of healthy life.
caption:
Burden of disease, 2021

Disability-Adjusted Life Years (DALYs) per 100,000 individuals from all causes. DALYs measure the total burden of disease – both from years of life lost due to premature death and years lived with a disability. One DALY equals one lost year of healthy life.

The choropleth map displays the global distribution of the burden of disease in 2021, measured as Disability-Adjusted Life Years per 100,000 individuals. The color scale ranges from light beige for the lowest values near zero to deep maroon for the highest values exceeding 60,000 DALYs. The highest burden of disease is concentrated in sub-Saharan Africa, where countries such as the Central African Republic, Chad, and Somalia fall into the darkest red band, indicating values above 60,000 DALYs per 100,000 people. Other nations in this high-burden category include Guinea, Mali, and Mozambique. Moving outward from this central cluster, countries in Eastern Europe and parts of Central Asia, including Russia and Ukraine, appear in the medium-dark red bands, representing values between 40,000 and 50,000 DALYs. In contrast, the lowest burden of disease is visible across Western Europe, North America, and Australia, where nations like Norway, Canada, and the United States are shaded in light beige, corresponding to values below 20,000 DALYs. South American countries generally occupy the lower-middle range of the scale, with shades of orange indicating values between 20,000 and 30,000 DALYs. Greenland is marked with diagonal hatching to indicate no data is available. The visual pattern shows a distinct geographic gradient, with the most severe health burdens located in the tropical regions of Africa and the lightest burdens distributed across the high-income nations of the Northern Hemisphere and Oceania.
